description_long: This is a stovetop kettle, specifically resembling a moka pot, with a robust, slightly bulbous lower chamber and a more angular upper section. The exterior is predominantly gray with a speckled, stone-like texture, giving it a rugged, utilitarian appearance. The handle is thick, side-mounted, and appears to be made from a darker, matte material, possibly heat-resistant plastic or coated metal, with a rough, irregular surface. The lid is flat and circular with a central knob for easy removal, and the spout is short and integrated into the upper body for controlled pouring. A small metallic safety valve is visible on the side of the lower chamber, typical of moka pots. The overall design is functional, with minimal decorative elements, making it suitable for urban or industrial environments.
description: A rugged, gray, speckled stovetop kettle with a thick, dark handle, flat lid with knob, and a short pouring spout. Its utilitarian design features a visible safety valve and minimal decoration.
description_view_0: The kettle is shown from the side, highlighting the thick, dark, side-mounted handle and the flat lid with a central knob.
description_view_1: This view centers on the spout, showing its short, integrated design and the angular upper body of the kettle.
description_view_2: The safety valve on the lower chamber is visible, along with the handle and the overall bulbous shape of the kettle.
description_view_3: The back of the kettle is shown, focusing on the full length of the handle and the seam where the upper and lower chambers meet.
